title: Anglo-Iranian Oil Company (AIOC) Net Profits, UK Tax Payments, and Iran Royalty Payments, 1910-1951
source_org: US Bureau of Mines Information Circular 8203 (1963), Table 2 (p.17)
extraction_method: pdftoppm -r 150 render to PNG (PDF page 25) + Read tool visual verification. Column totals cross-checked against source-printed TOTAL row (244,222 / 154,544 / 119,767) -- matches.
frequency: annual (1910-32 aggregated as a single row in the source)
notes: The pre-1954 predecessor series to usbm1963-consortium-disbursements-1954-1962 -- together the two datasets span AIOC/Consortium oil-sector payments to Iran continuously from 1910 to 1962, a 52-year span, bridging the D'Arcy Concession era (1910-32), the 1933 revised concession (1933-51), the 1951-54 nationalization crisis (data gap, income from foreign sales 'negligible' per source narrative), and the post-1954 Consortium era. Genuinely complements (does not duplicate) the existing 'Pahlavi-era oil revenue/exports 1955/56-1970/71' series already in this project (per DATA_INVENTORY.md section 2) by reaching back an additional ~45 years earlier. War-years royalty guarantees (1939-43) are footnoted with both the guaranteed-minimum figure actually paid AND the lower counterfactual figure that would have applied without the guarantee -- both preserved in the notes column, not just the paid figure.
